heading: Rights of victims to attend and observe trial
label: 3510 Rights of victims to attend and observe trial
notes: References in TextSection 503(e)(2) of the Victims’ Rights and Restitution Act of 1990, referred to in subsec. (c), is classified to , Crime Control and Law Enforcement.section 20141(e)(2) of Title 34

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–6, § 2(d)111 Stat. 13Mar. 19, 1997“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending ] shall apply in cases pending on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 3593 of this titleMar. 19, 1997
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–6, § 2(d)111 Stat. 13Mar. 19, 1997“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending ] shall apply in cases pending on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 3593 of this titleMar. 19, 1997

children.heading: .—Non-Capital Cases
children.label: a .—Non-Capital Cases
children.text: Notwithstanding any statute, rule, or other provision of law, a United States district court shall not order any victim of an offense excluded from the trial of a defendant accused of that offense because such victim may, during the sentencing hearing, make a statement or present any information in relation to the sentence.

children.heading: .—Capital Cases
children.label: b .—Capital Cases
children.text: Notwithstanding any statute, rule, or other provision of law, a United States district court shall not order any victim of an offense excluded from the trial of a defendant accused of that offense because such victim may, during the sentencing hearing, testify as to the effect of the offense on the victim and the victim’s family or as to any other factor for which notice is required under section 3593(a).

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: c .—Definition
children.text: As used in this section, the term “victim” includes all persons defined as victims in section 503(e)(2) of the Victims’ Rights and Restitution Act of 1990.
